e2e.purpose: End-to-end test suite that runs against a live OpenMeter stack bootstrapped via Docker Compose. Tests call the real HTTP API through the generated Go client (v1) and a hand-rolled v3Client (v3), asserting full-stack behavior including async Kafka→ClickHouse pipelines. No internal domain packages are imported — only HTTP clients and public type definitions.

patterns.name: OPENMETER_ADDRESS env-gate
patterns.description: Every test file calls initClient(t) or newV3Client(t), both of which call t.Skip when OPENMETER_ADDRESS env var is not set. New tests must never bypass this guard.
patterns.example: address := os.Getenv("OPENMETER_ADDRESS"); if address == "" { t.Skip("OPENMETER_ADDRESS not set") }

patterns.name: assert.EventuallyWithT for async pipeline assertions
patterns.description: Any assertion on ClickHouse data (meter queries, entitlement balances) must use assert.EventuallyWithT with a 1-minute timeout and 1-second interval. Kafka→sink-worker→ClickHouse is async; never assert synchronously after ingest.
patterns.example: assert.EventuallyWithT(t, func(t *assert.CollectT) { resp, _ := client.QueryMeterWithResponse(...) }, time.Minute, time.Second)

patterns.name: v3Client typed wrapper (status, body, problem) triple
patterns.description: v3 API tests use *v3Client from v3helpers_test.go, not the generated SDK. Methods return (statusCode, *T, *v3Problem). Assertions check statusCode first, then the typed body. New v3 tests must follow the same triple pattern.
patterns.example: status, plan, problem := c.CreatePlan(body); require.Equal(t, http.StatusCreated, status, "problem: %+v", problem)

patterns.name: Unique keys via timestamp/ULID to avoid shared-DB collisions
patterns.description: Feature keys, plan keys, addon keys, and customer keys use a timestamp or ULID suffix (via uniqueKey() helper). Never use hardcoded keys for entities that cannot be idempotently re-created.

patterns.name: shouldRunSlowTests guard for minute-resolution assertions
patterns.description: Tests that sleep >5s (waiting for a new minute for entitlement reset or credit granularity) must be gated behind shouldRunSlowTests(t), which checks RUN_SLOW_TESTS env var.

patterns.name: assertProblemDetail / assertValidationCode / assertInvalidParameterRule for error paths
patterns.description: Negative-path tests must use the v3helpers assertion helpers. assertValidationCode checks extensions.validationErrors[].code; assertInvalidParameterRule checks schema-layer invalid_parameters[].rule; assertProblemDetail checks a substring in Detail.

patterns.name: Meter definitions in e2e/config.yaml before test ingest
patterns.description: Every event type ingested by tests must have a corresponding meter slug defined in e2e/config.yaml. The sink-worker validates events against known meters and silently drops events to unknown meters.
patterns.example:
meters:
  - slug: plan_meter
    eventType: plan_meter
    aggregation: SUM

key_file_guides.role: Provides initClient (v1 SDK client) and shouldRunSlowTests. Single source of truth for test bootstrapping and env-var guards.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding test setup logic here that skips rather than fails will silently ignore tests in CI when the env var is missing.

key_file_guides.role: Defines *v3Client (raw HTTP), v3Problem (RFC 7807 parse), decodeTyped generic, all typed CRUD wrappers for plans/addons/planaddons, validXxx fixture builders, and assertion helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: v3Client.do uses t.Context() timeout (v3RequestTimeout). Do not bypass with context.Background(). uniqueKey() helper is defined here — use it for all entity keys.

key_file_guides.role: CreateCustomerWithSubject (creates customer then upserts subject), GetMeterIDBySlug, QueryMeterV3 (raw HTTP v3 meter query). Shared cross-test helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: QueryMeterV3 uses raw http.DefaultClient and reads OPENMETER_ADDRESS directly — it will skip if the address is empty. Do not inline these helpers in test files.

key_file_guides.role: OpenMeter config injected into the Docker Compose stack. credits.enabled=false — tests in productcatalog_test.go (TestSettlementMode) depend on this being false.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new meter slug in a test without adding the corresponding meter definition here causes the sink-worker to silently drop events. credits.enabled=false is a deliberate test contract — do not change without updating tests.

key_file_guides.role: test-local target: tears down, brings up compose stack, waits for sink-worker /healthz on port 30000, then runs go test with TZ=UTC OPENMETER_ADDRESS=http://localhost:38888.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TZ=UTC is essential for deterministic time-window assertions. Port 38888 is the openmeter API; port 30000 is the sink-worker healthz endpoint.

key_file_guides.role: Overrides image: with build: .. so local tests use the locally built binary, not a released image.
key_file_guides.watch_for: docker-compose.openmeter-latest.yaml uses the published GHCR image for CI — do not merge the two approaches.

key_file_guides.role: Tests the openmeter-jobs ledger backfill-accounts command via docker compose exec and verifies ledger_customer_accounts rows via direct pgxpool queries.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ensureLocalComposeBackfillSupport skips when OPENMETER_ADDRESS is not the localhost:38888 compose stack — this test cannot run against remote envs.

anti_patterns: Asserting meter query results synchronously after ingest without assert.EventuallyWithT — data flows async through Kafka and ClickHouse.
Using hardcoded feature/plan/customer keys shared across test functions — causes 409 or silent state pollution in a shared persistent DB.
Importing app/common, Wire provider packages, or Ent adapters — e2e tests must only use the HTTP clients and public type constants.
Adding new meters to tests without a corresponding entry in e2e/config.yaml — the sink-worker silently drops events to unknown meters.
Using port numbers in the 30000 range for new services without verifying they do not conflict with the root docker-compose.yaml or quickstart (40000 range).

decisions.decision: Two client layers: generated api.ClientWithResponses for v1 and a hand-rolled v3Client for v3.
decisions.rationale: The v3 API was still evolving when these tests were written. A thin hand-rolled wrapper returning (status, *T, *v3Problem) gives tests direct control over HTTP-level assertions without fighting the generated SDK's union-type machinery.

E2e tests for settlement-mode validation (TestSettlementMode in productcatalog_test.go) depend on credits being off to assert that credit_only settlement mode is rejected. This is a deliberate, documented contract.

decisions.decision: Separate docker-compose.openmeter-local.yaml overriding image: with build: for local runs.
decisions.rationale: Makes 'make test-local' always test the locally built binary while CI can swap in a published image via docker-compose.openmeter-latest.yaml without changing test code.

code_examples.scenario: Writing a new v3 resource lifecycle test (create → assert status → use resource ID in subsequent steps)

quickstart.purpose: Self-contained demo environment that boots all OpenMeter services (openmeter, sink-worker, balance-worker, notification-service, billing-worker, openmeter-jobs) via Docker Compose and validates end-to-end ingest→query behavior with quickstart_test.go. Uses port range 40000–49999 to avoid conflicts with the root dev environment and the e2e suite (30000 range).

patterns.name: OPENMETER_ADDRESS env-gate on tests
patterns.description: Both initClient and openmeterAddress check OPENMETER_ADDRESS and call t.Skip if unset. Tests are never run against an embedded server, only against a live Docker Compose stack.
patterns.example: address := os.Getenv("OPENMETER_ADDRESS"); if address == "" { t.Skip("OPENMETER_ADDRESS not set") }

patterns.name: assert.EventuallyWithT for async ingest assertions
patterns.description: All ingest-then-query assertions use assert.EventuallyWithT with a 30-second timeout and 1-second tick to tolerate Kafka/ClickHouse propagation latency.
patterns.example: assert.EventuallyWithT(t, func(t *assert.CollectT) { resp := queryMeterV3(...); require.Len(t, resp.Data, 2) }, 30*time.Second, time.Second)

patterns.name: Port range 40000–49999
patterns.description: All Docker Compose service ports in this folder use the 40000 range to avoid collisions with the root dev docker-compose.yaml and the e2e suite (30000 range). Application ports: openmeter=48888, sink-worker=40000, balance-worker=40001, notification-service=40002, billing-worker=40003.

patterns.name: Separate docker-compose.debug-ports.yaml overlay
patterns.description: docker-compose.debug-ports.yaml adds host port bindings for Kafka/ClickHouse/Redis/Postgres/Svix only when debugging. The base docker-compose.yaml exposes only application-level ports. make test-local merges both files.

patterns.name: Unique meter slugs per test run via timestamp suffix
patterns.description: quickstart_test.go generates v1MeterSlug and v3MeterKey with a UnixNano suffix to avoid collisions on shared persistent DB state across runs.

patterns.name: autoMigrate: migration in config.yaml
patterns.description: Uses the Atlas SQL migration path (autoMigrate: migration), not the development-only ent schema create (autoMigrate: ent). This exercises the real production migration pipeline.

key_file_guides.role: End-to-end smoke test: creates meters via v1 and v3 APIs, ingests CloudEvents with fixed IDs (00001/00002/00003), asserts meter query values for both API versions using EventuallyWithT.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses context.Background() in helper functions — acceptable here as integration test, not application code. Meter IDs and event IDs are intentionally fixed (not random) since the test expects specific count values (2 + 1 window split).

key_file_guides.role: Full-stack compose: all five OpenMeter binaries plus infra (Kafka, ClickHouse, Redis, Postgres, Svix). References docker-compose.base.yaml services via extends.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New binaries added to the main stack must also be added here with matching command, config volume mount, and depends_on health checks. openmeter-jobs runs 'quickstart cron' (not a long-running server) — do not add a healthcheck to it.

key_file_guides.role: Minimal production-like config: three sample meters (api_requests_total, api_requests_duration, tokens_total), Svix configured, portal enabled with tokenSecret, autoMigrate: migration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If new required config fields are added to app/config, this file must be updated or the quickstart stack silently fails to start. The Svix apiKey is a test JWT — not sensitive.

key_file_guides.role: test-local: spin up stack (both compose files), health-check sink-worker port 40000, run tests with OPENMETER_ADDRESS=http://localhost:48888, tear down.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Health check hits sink-worker port 40000 (not the openmeter API port 48888). TZ is not set here (unlike e2e) — tests use fixed UTC timestamps in the event data instead.

anti_patterns: Using port numbers outside the 40000 range — they conflict with the root docker-compose.yaml dev environment or the e2e suite.
Running quickstart_test.go without a live Docker Compose stack — tests must guard with t.Skip on missing OPENMETER_ADDRESS.
Hardcoding event IDs or meter slugs without a unique suffix — the test uses fixed event IDs (00001/00002/00003) intentionally; new tests must use unique suffixes.
Omitting depends_on health checks when adding a new service to docker-compose.yaml — services start before dependencies are ready.
Setting autoMigrate to 'ent' instead of 'migration' — this bypasses the Atlas migration pipeline and hides migration regressions.

decisions.decision: quickstart_test.go uses the generated Go SDK (api/client/go) for v1 but raw HTTP helpers for v3.
decisions.rationale: Demonstrates the intended SDK usage for v1 to new users while covering the v3 API surface that lacks a fully generated client helper matching the test's assertion patterns.

decisions.decision: All five OpenMeter binaries (openmeter, sink-worker, balance-worker, notification-service, billing-worker) plus openmeter-jobs are declared in docker-compose.yaml.
decisions.rationale: Quickstart is the canonical smoke-test for new operators; it must demonstrate the full production deployment topology, not a reduced subset.

decisions.decision: Port range 40000–49999 reserved exclusively for quickstart.
decisions.rationale: Avoids conflicts with both the root dev environment (standard ports) and the e2e suite (30000 range), allowing all three environments to run simultaneously on the same machine.

code_examples.scenario: Assert meter query value after async ingest using EventuallyWithT (the core test pattern)
